AMBIL, BILIRAN, BORACAY (photos), GUIMARAS (photos), LUZON: Camarines Sur, NCR, Pangasinan (photos), Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Davao Occidental, Davao del Norte, Davao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, Surigao, MINDORO, NEGROS (photos), PALAUI, PANAY (photos), SAMAR, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: Widespread in the Philippines. Confined to back mangrove and banks of tidal streams and estuaries. 0-3m.

species.citation: (L.) Kurz , Forest Fl. Burma 2 (1877) 237 ; Vent., Dec. Gen. Nov. (1808) 8, nom. inval.; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 428; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 444; Steenis, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 128; Raganas et al., Biodiversitas 26 (2025) 1193; ≡ Bignonia indica L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 625.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nepal, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. BANTON, BASILAN, GUIMARAS, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra (photos), Bataan, Cagayan, Laguna, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Davao Oriental (photos), Davao del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY.
species.notes: Luzon (Cagayan) to Palawan and Mindanao, in most or all islands and provinces. A characteristic, short-lived nomad tree, nowhere gregarious, never in mature rainforest but always in openings, secondary growths and thickets, rather indifferent to climate and soils. 0-1000m.

species.notes: Ultramafic forests, usually near the coast.

species.citation: (Blume) Miq. , Ann. Mus. Bot. Lugduno-Batavi 3 (1867) 250 ; Steenis, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 155; Fernando et al., Forest Flora of Dinagat (2017) 88; Cortez et al., Check List 19 (2023) 987; ≡ Spathodea gigantea Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 761. = Radermachera biternata Merr. , PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 238 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 445. = Radermachera elliptica Merr. , PJS 3c (1908) 334 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 445. = Radermachera elmeri Merr. , Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 29 (1905) 48 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 445. = Radermachera fragrans (Elmer) Steenis , Recueil Trav. Bot. Néerl. 24 (1927) 996; ≡ Radermachera elmeri var. fragrans Elmer , LPB 7 (1915) 2561 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 446; Type: Elmer 12681, Palawan: Brooks Point, Addison Peak, Feb-1911. = Radermachera palawanensis Merr. , PJS 3c (1908) 336 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 446. = Radermachera sibuyanensis Elmer , LPB 4 (1912) 1485 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 447.
species.distribution: Borneo, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Myanmar, Philippines, Sumatra. BUCAS GRANDE (photos), BUSUANGA, CULION, DINAGAT, LUZON: Aurora, Benguet, Bulacan, Cagayan, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Mountain Province, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, PALAWAN, PANAY, SIARGAO, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: Primary and secondary forests, in various soil types including ultramafic-derived, also in areas subject to a dry monsoon. Not rarely a pioneer in secondary forest, cuttings sometimes used for strengthening terracing on unstable slopes. Early flowering and fruiting as a shrub. 0-1600m.

species.citation: (Blume) Miq. , Ann. Mus. Bot. Lugduno-Batavi 3 (1867) 250 ; Steenis, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 152; ≡ Spathodea glandulosa Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 762.
species.distribution: China, India, Java, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand.

species.citation: (Blanco) Seem. , J. Bot. 8 (1870) 147, as 'quadripinna' ; Turner, Phytotaxa 71 (2012) 34; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific Biodiv. 17 (2024) 10; ≡ Stereospermum quadripinnatum (Blanco) Fern.-Vill. , Fl. Filip., 3rd Ed. 1 (1877) 151; ≡ Millingtonia quadripinnata Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 501; Type: Cuming 1182 (K, neo; CGE, K, MO, P, iso), Luzon: Albay. = Stereospermum seemannii Rolfe , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 21 (1884) 314. = Millingtonia pinnata Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 501, nom. illeg., non Roxb.; ≡ Radermachera pinnata Seem. , J. Bot. 8 (1870) 147 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 446; Merrill, PJS 3c (1908) 337; Steenis, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 153; Type: Merrill Species Blancoanae 834 (leg. M. Ramos) (A, neo; BO, GH, K, L, MO, US, iso), Luzon: Rizal, Bosoboso, 9-Mar-1915. = Radermachera pinnata var. glabra Merr. , PJS 3c (1908) 337; Type: FB 2671 Ahern's collector, Luzon: Rizal, Bosoboso, Jan-1905. ssp. quadripinnata. = Radermachera acuminata Merr., PJS 3c (1908) 335; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 445. = Radermachera brachybotrys Merr., PJS 26 (1925) 489; Merrill, EPFP 4 (1926) 251; Type: BS 41688 Edaño, Leyte. = Radermachera fenicis Merr., PJS 3c (1908) 335; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 446. = Radermachera mindorensis Merr., PJS 3c (1908) 338; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 446. = Radermachera whitfordii Merr., PJS 7c (1912) 352; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 447. Distribution: Moluccas, Philippines, Sulawesi. BILIRAN, CAMIGUIN, LEYTE, LUZON: Cagayan, MINDANAO, MINDORO, NEGROS, SAMAR. Notes: In Luzon from Cagayan to Bicol Peninsula. Secondary and primary forests, along streams, common. 0-600m. Native. ssp. lobbii (Teijsm. & Binn.) I.M.Turner, Reinwardtia 16 (2017) 47; ≡ Spathodea lobbii Teijsm. & Binn., Natuurk. Tijdschr. Ned.-Indië 25 (1863) 413; Type: from Singapore. = Radermachera quadripinnata ssp. acuminata (Steenis) I.M.Turner, Phytotaxa 71 (2012) 34; ≡ Radermachera pinnata ssp. acuminata (Steenis) Steenis, Blumea 23 (1976) 129; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 154; ≡ Radermachera lobbii ssp. acuminata Steenis, Bull. Jard. Bot. Buitenzorg, 3rd Sér. 10 (1928) 247; Type: from Indonesia. Distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand. CULION, PALAWAN. Notes: Low elevation open grasslands in Palawan and Culion. Native.
species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Nicobar Isls, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. BILIRAN, BOHOL, CAMIGUIN, CEBU, CULION, HOMONHON, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Bataan, Benguet, Cagayan, Camarines Sur, Cavite, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Mountain Province, NCR, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Lanao, Lanao del Sur, Misamis, Zamboanga, Zamboanga Sibugay, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, POLILLO, SAMAR.
